definition: Applying the Minsky taxonomy to covenant-lite credit markets: covenant-lite eliminates the speculative finance phase, creating a direct hedge-to-Ponzi transition. This implies credit cascade dynamics could be more binary (cliff-edge) than historically observed. The specific timeline compression (3-6 months) was refuted as empirically uncalibrated.
evidence: Debate pair_1 called this the strongest theoretical contribution
Covenant-lite eliminates early warning mechanisms that marked speculative phase
Zero organic downturn observations for covenant-lite regime
Specific 3-6 month timeline refuted as asserted without empirical basis
open_questions: Does covenant-lite produce cliff-edge or gradual defaults in organic downturn?
How does bimodal vs smooth default distribution affect loss severity models?
